message:
fix(auth): eliminate TOCTOU race in device ID file creation

Both src/auth.rs and src/providers/anthropic.rs had a time-of-check to
time-of-use (TOCTOU) race in the kimi_device_id() function where the
device ID file was created with fs::write() followed by a separate
fs::set_permissions() call, leaving a window where the file existed with
default permissions (potentially world-readable) before being locked down.

Fix:
- Replace fs::write() + fs::set_permissions() with OpenOptions using
  create_new(true) to atomically create the file only if it doesn't exist,
  combined with mode(0o600) via OpenOptionsExt on Unix to set restrictive
  permissions at creation time rather than after the fact
- In src/providers/anthropic.rs, additionally replace create(true).truncate(true)
  with create_new(true) to match the same atomic-creation pattern and avoid
  overwriting an existing device ID file that another process may have created
  concurrently

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
